beg.desc: Rust programs start with the fn main() function. print! macros output text.
beg.usecase: The basic entry point. Compile with: rustc main.rs

int.desc: Organize code and track packages using Cargo projects.
int.usecase: Structuring standard systems and importing external libraries.

pro.desc: Control compiler checks by configuring feature flags inside dependency scopes.
pro.usecase: Writing cross-platform system features at compile time.

beg.desc: Variables are immutable by default. Use 'mut' to make them mutable.
beg.usecase: Defining variables safely with compiler-checked immutability.

int.desc: Use variable shadowing to transform values while keeping variables immutable.
int.usecase: Parsing data inputs cleanly without defining new temporary variables.

pro.desc: Declare constants with explicit type signatures for global values.
pro.usecase: Defining global configuration parameters with static scope.

beg.desc: Rust has basic scalar types: integers, floats, booleans, and characters.

int.desc: Work with compound types: tuples and arrays.

pro.desc: Use fixed-width integers and custom numeric conversions.

beg.desc: Rust prevents memory bugs by checking values ownership rules at compile time.
beg.usecase: Writing memory-safe code without garbage collectors.

int.desc: Use clone to create deep copies of heap values when move is not desired.
int.usecase: Explicit duplication of structures in memory.

pro.desc: Implement the Copy trait on custom types to allow automatic duplication.

beg.desc: Borrow references to values using & to read data without taking ownership.

int.desc: Borrow values mutably using &mut, allowing changes to the target data.
int.usecase: Passing references that mutate target models.

pro.desc: Understand borrow rules: only one mutable reference OR multiple read references are allowed.
pro.usecase: Guarding against data races at compile time.

beg.desc: Slices are references to a contiguous sequence of elements in a collection.

int.desc: Use array slices to reference sub-parts of integer arrays.

pro.desc: Pass string slices (&str) to APIs to accept both String and literal values.

beg.desc: Structs allow grouping fields to model custom object schemas.

int.desc: Define methods inside impl blocks to attach behavior to structs.

pro.desc: Build builder patterns or static constructors using associated functions.

beg.desc: Enums represent variables that can hold one of several variants.

int.desc: Embed values or structures directly inside Enum variants.

pro.desc: Write exhaustive matches, forcing developers to handle all cases.

beg.desc: Use Option to represent values that can be Some or None (null replacement).

int.desc: Use if-let to unwrap optionals cleanly without full match statements.

pro.desc: Chain optional operations using map, and_then, and unwrap_or.

beg.desc: Vectors are resizable dynamic arrays. Create them with the vec! macro.

int.desc: Safely access vector elements using the .get() method returning Options.
int.usecase: Safe indexing that prevents out-of-bounds crashes.

pro.desc: Iterate and mutate elements in vectors using mutable references.

beg.desc: HashMaps store key-value associations. Import from std::collections.

int.desc: Read properties safely and handle entry modifications using entry API.
int.usecase: Inserting default values if a key doesn't exist.

pro.desc: Process frequency updates dynamically using mutable dereferencing.

beg.desc: Use Result<T, E> for operations that can succeed (Ok) or fail (Err).

int.desc: Propagate errors up the call stack using the ? operator.

pro.desc: Transform and map errors using map_err and custom error classes.

beg.desc: Generics parameterize structures or functions to run with any type.

int.desc: Create generic structs that can hold fields of dynamic types.

pro.desc: Define generic implementations constrained to run only under specific type bounds.
pro.usecase: Restricting generic classes to printable types.

beg.desc: Traits define shared interface behaviors. Like interfaces in Java.

int.desc: Use trait bounds to restrict generic parameters on functions.

pro.desc: Define dynamic polymorphic collections using trait objects (Box<dyn Trait>).

beg.desc: Lifetimes ensure references remain valid for as long as they are used.

int.desc: Use explicit lifetime annotations when returning references from functions.

pro.desc: Structs that hold references must declare explicit lifetimes.
pro.usecase: Ensuring containing structures don't outlive references.

beg.desc: Closures are anonymous functions that can capture values from their scope.

int.desc: Capture environment variables using closures, referencing external variables.
int.usecase: Using dynamic parameters from outer blocks.

pro.desc: Move ownership of variables into closures using the 'move' keyword.
pro.usecase: Preparing closures to be sent to separate threads.

beg.desc: Iterators allow performing actions on sequences of elements.

int.desc: Map and transform lists using lazy iterator adapters.

pro.desc: Build custom structures that implement the Iterator trait.

beg.desc: Box<T> allocates values on the heap instead of the stack.

int.desc: Use Box to define recursive data structures with compiler-known sizes.
int.usecase: Modeling dynamic recursive models like trees or graphs.

pro.desc: Enable compiler dereference coercions using Box wrappers.

beg.desc: Rc enables multiple reference-counted owners in single-threaded code.

int.desc: Use Arc (Atomic Reference Counted) to share structures across threads safely.

pro.desc: Combine Arc and Mutex to modify shared data across multiple threads.

beg.desc: Spawn threads in Rust to execute code concurrently.

int.desc: Send values between threads using safe FIFO messaging channels.

pro.desc: Verify program compilation safety against data races at compile time.

beg.desc: Async/await allows non-blocking asynchronous coding (needs tokio runtime).

int.desc: Perform concurrent operations in parallel using tokio::join! macros.

pro.desc: Select fast task completions using select! statements.
pro.usecase: Implementing time limits or race tasks.

beg.desc: Read and write files easily using std::fs methods.

int.desc: Perform buffered read operations on large files for high performance.

pro.desc: Perform non-blocking async file operations with tokio::fs.

beg.desc: Organize code using modules (mod keyword) and split file directories.

int.desc: Use pub use to re-export items, creating clean API facades.
int.code: pub use utils::help; // exposes helper at root level

pro.desc: Declare external dependencies and compiler feature flags inside Cargo.toml.

beg.desc: Unsafe blocks bypass compiler rules to execute raw memory operations.

int.desc: Call foreign functions (C libraries) using FFI interface bindings.

pro.desc: Create unsafe traits and implement raw memory allocators.
pro.code:
unsafe trait Shared {}
unsafe impl Shared for Point {}
pro.usecase: Writing custom system allocators or drivers.

beg.desc: Write unit tests in the same file using #[cfg(test)] attributes.
beg.usecase: Unit testing modules. Run with: cargo test

int.desc: Verify exception triggers using #[should_panic] validations.

pro.desc: Write integration tests inside a separate /tests folder directory.
pro.code:
// tests/integration.rs
// Test application binaries from outside consumer scopes
